verdict.label: likely_ai_generated
rationale: No strong metadata signal was found.
Model Ateeqq/ai-vs-human-image-detector returned labels: ai=1.000, hum=0.000.
Calibration: AI threshold 0.72, real threshold 0.28, reliability weight 0.72.
Model dima806/ai_vs_real_image_detection returned labels: FAKE=0.686, REAL=0.314.
Calibration: AI threshold 0.82, real threshold 0.22, reliability weight 0.35.
Model jacoballessio/ai-image-detect-distilled returned labels: fake=0.614, real=0.386.

summary.headline: The image has signals consistent with AI-generated or synthetic media.
summary.plain_language: Verdict: likely_ai_generated with medium confidence. Estimated AI probability is 79%; manipulation probability is 25%. Review the evidence layers before taking action.
limitations: Image authenticity cannot be proven from pixels alone.
Social platforms often strip metadata, so missing EXIF is not proof of AI generation.
No login scraping, private account access, face-search, or private identity inference was performed.

decision_trace: Final label is likely_ai_generated with medium confidence.
Layer ledger: 1 support AI, 1 support real/camera-origin, 0 support manipulation, 2 weak anomalies, 6 neutral/inconclusive.

decision_support.plain_summary: The AI-generated verdict is mainly driven by visual detector evidence, then checked against metadata and forensic layers.
primary_drivers: Visual Model Consensus Layer: supports_ai_generated (AI 79%, manipulation 25%).
counter_evidence: Compression And ELA Layer: supports_real_or_camera_origin (AI 2%, manipulation 24%).
uncertainty_factors: No signed C2PA/content credential was available to independently confirm provenance.
2 layer(s) produced weak anomaly signals that need context.
what_would_help: Original, uncompressed media from the device or platform export.
Public source URL, timestamps, captions, and account/page context.
Manual review when the result affects safety, reputation, or legal action.

evidence: 3/3 visual models leaned AI-generated at or above 60%.
Skipped portrait-only model SadraCoding/SDXL-Deepfake-Detector; portrait likelihood was 0.46.
Central skin-tone ratio: 0.681; side skin-tone ratio: 0.935.
No major input-quality risk flags were detected.
Model dima806/ai_vs_real_image_detection returned labels: FAKE=0.686, REAL=0.314.
Calibration: AI threshold 0.82, real threshold 0.22, reliability weight 0.35.
Model jacoballessio/ai-image-detect-distilled returned labels: fake=0.614, real=0.386.

likely_ai_requires: Readable generative metadata or C2PA declaration,
or calibrated multi-model consensus with no model voting real/human-origin.
false_positive_controls: Raw model scores are reliability-weighted before aggregation.
Detector disagreement and real/human votes force abstention.
Portrait-specialist models only run after a portrait-likelihood gate.
Low-quality crops, screenshots, and compressed exports cap confidence.

evidence: No major input-quality risk flags were detected.

regional_evidence_map.interpretation: This abstract map compares regions against the rest of the same image. It does not display the uploaded image and does not identify what caused a regional difference.

layers.name: Source, Metadata, And Provenance
layers.question: Does the file carry provenance or generation/editing metadata?
layers.method: EXIF/XMP/PNG text extraction plus optional C2PA manifest inspection.
limitations: Most social platforms strip metadata.
Metadata can be added, removed, or forged.

layers.name: Input Quality And Robustness
layers.question: Is the file quality strong enough for high-confidence automated detection?
layers.method: Check resolution, file size, aspect ratio, entropy, and format conditions known to affect detector reliability.
evidence: No major input-quality risk flags were detected.
No major input-quality risk flags were detected.

limitations: Low quality does not mean fake.
This layer adjusts confidence; it is not an AI detector by itself.

layers.name: Visual Model Consensus Layer
layers.question: Do pretrained visual detectors classify the image as AI-generated?
layers.method: Run configured Hugging Face image-classification detectors and aggregate votes.
evidence: Model Ateeqq/ai-vs-human-image-detector returned labels: ai=1.000, hum=0.000.
Model dima806/ai_vs_real_image_detection returned labels: FAKE=0.686, REAL=0.314.
Model jacoballessio/ai-image-detect-distilled returned labels: fake=0.614, real=0.386.
limitations: Pretrained detectors can fail on new generators, screenshots, crops, or heavy compression.
This layer is stronger when multiple models agree and weaker when they disagree.

layers.name: Luminance Layer
layers.question: Do brightness/contrast statistics look unusually flat, clipped, or over-regularized?
layers.method: Analyze grayscale entropy, contrast, clipping, and local brightness variation.
limitations: Studio lighting, compression, and screenshots can look smooth or clipped.
This layer is a weak supporting signal only.

layers.name: Chroma And Color Layer
layers.question: Do color statistics show unusually uniform or synthetic-looking saturation?
layers.method: Analyze RGB channel spread, channel correlation, and saturation distribution.
limitations: Color grading and camera profiles can dominate this signal.
This does not identify which generator, if any, created the image.

layers.name: Edge And Geometry Layer
layers.question: Are edges too inconsistent, too clean, or locally unnatural?
layers.method: Run edge extraction and compare edge density/variance across tiles.
limitations: Texture-rich real scenes and bokeh-heavy portraits can both skew edge statistics.
This is anomaly evidence, not identity or attribution evidence.

layers.name: Noise Residual Layer
layers.question: Is sensor-like noise consistent across image regions?
layers.method: Estimate edge/noise residual variance across a 4x4 tile grid.
limitations: Denoising, resizing, and platform recompression can erase normal sensor noise.
Generated images can also contain synthetic noise.

layers.name: Compression And ELA Layer
layers.question: Do recompression artifacts suggest editing, screenshots, or pasted regions?
layers.method: Recompress image to JPEG quality 90 and measure error-level deltas plus JPEG markers.
limitations: ELA is fragile and often reacts to normal recompression.
A clean ELA result does not prove authenticity.

layers.name: Frequency Spectrum Layer
layers.question: Does the image have unusual high-frequency or overly smooth spectral structure?
layers.method: Compute grayscale FFT energy distribution across low/mid/high frequency bands.
limitations: Frequency patterns are affected by camera sharpening, resizing, and compression.
This layer is useful for consistency checks, not stand-alone classification.

layers.name: Tile Region Anomaly Layer
layers.question: Do any local regions behave differently from the rest of the image?
layers.method: Split the image into a 4x4 grid and compare brightness/noise/edge residual z-scores.
evidence: Region map: 16 tiles analyzed; 2 high-severity regional differences.
Top anomalous tiles: row 3 col 2 score 5.8179, row 4 col 2 score 4.4072, row 4 col 3 score 4.0364, row 3 col 3 score 3.98.
limitations: This identifies suspicious regions, not the cause.
Natural subject/background boundaries often produce regional differences.

explainability.calibration_note: Scores are calibrated for cautious triage, not certainty. Agreement across multiple visual detectors is stronger than any single model score.

reproducibility.pipeline: metadata + C2PA/provenance + perceptual hashes + compression/noise checks + pretrained open-source model ensemble
reproducibility.model_training: No custom model was trained by this application.

next_steps: Preserve the original file and URLs if this may become evidence.
Use platform reporting tools for non-consensual intimate imagery or impersonation.
Treat the verdict as an evidence summary, not a legal or forensic certificate.
